content.full_text:
Farn PoLrrrcAL PnecrrcEs CorraMrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
June 30, 2003
Harry A. Krug, Director
Air Quality Standards
Department o f Agriculture
100 Sunrise Blvd., Suite F
Colusa, CA95932
Re:
Your Request for Assistance
Our FiIe No. G-03-133
Dear Mr. Krug:
This leffer is in response to your request for assistance regarding the conflict of
interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the o'Act').I Your request is a
protest of the Sacramento Valley Basinwide Air Pollution Control Councii(. B CC,,),
which includes your position in their conflict of interest code.
As we discussed in our telephone conversation of June 24,2003, it appears you
are requesting that BCC amend their code to exclude your position from it. Siction
87307 of the Act provides:
'An agency may at any time amend its Conflict of Interest
Code, subject to the provisions of Section 87303, either upon its
own initiative or in response to a petition submitted by an officer,
employee, member or consultant of the agency, or a resident of the
jurisdiction. If the agency fails to act upon such a petition within
ninety days, the petition shall be deerned denied. within thirty days
after the denial of a petition, the petitioner rnay appeal to the code
reviewing body. The code reviewing body shall either dismiss the
appeal or issue an appropriate order to the agency within ninety
days."
' Gove-ment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


FileNo. W-03-133
Page No. 2
o
While not explicitly set forth in a single statute or regulation, the process for
petitioning to amend the BCC's conflict of interest code is as follows:
Permissive changes to the conflict of interest code may be made on BCC's own
motion, or upon a petition filed by an officer, employee, member or consultant of
BCC, or a resident of the jurisdiction.
If BCC fails to act upon a petition within 90 days, the petition is deemed denied.
You may appeal BCC's denial of the petition by filing an appeal within 30 days of
the agency's denial. This appeal is filed with the code reviewing body (the FPPC).
The FPPC shall either dismiss the appeal or issue an appropriate order to BCC within
90 days ofreceipt ofappeal.
The FPPC's order will then be subject to judicial review upon a pleading filed by the
FPPC, BCC, by any officer employee, member or consultant of BCC, or by a resident
of the jurisdiction.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Jwwjg

sections.question_synthetic: What is the process for petitioning to amend a conflict of interest code to exclude a position, and what are the rights of appeal if the agency denies the petition?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: An officer, employee, member or consultant may petition their agency to amend its conflict of interest code. If the agency does not act within 90 days, the petition is deemed denied, and the petitioner may appeal to the FPPC within 30 days. The FPPC must issue an order within 90 days, which is subject to judicial review.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers, but contains clear legal question and procedural answer in letter format. File number notation shows 'W-03-133' on page 2 but document ID is G-03-133 in header (W may indicate 'writley' or administrative code). Extracted synthetic versions from body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the process for petitioning to amend a conflict of interest code to exclude a position, and what are the rights of appeal if the agency denies the petition?

CONCLUSION: An officer, employee, member or consultant may petition their agency to amend its conflict of interest code. If the agency does not act within 90 days, the petition is deemed denied, and the petitioner may appeal to the FPPC within 30 days. The FPPC must issue an order within 90 days, which is subject to judicial review.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to Harry Krug's request regarding the process for petitioning the Sacramento Valley Basinwide Air Pollution Control Council to amend its conflict of interest code to exclude his position. The letter explains the statutory procedures for code amendment petitions, denial timelines, and appeal rights.
